name: Reading Vocal Tone for Movement, Resistance, and Sincerity
description: On a voice call, content is a fraction of the signal. Pitch, pace, breath, throat-clearing, hesitation, and inflection carry the rep's real position. This skill teaches what to listen FOR and what each pattern means tactically.
disclaimer: Vocal cues are probabilistic, not deterministic. A throat-clear before a number suggests uncertainty about that number, but it could also be allergies. Treat cues as Bayesian updates, not proofs.

context.when_to_use: Any voice negotiation. Most critical during numeric exchanges (price, term, fee) where the rep's tone reveals whether the number is firm or soft.
preconditions: Voice channel with good audio quality — bad VOIP destroys paralinguistic signal.
You can attend to tone WITHOUT losing the content thread. This requires practice; if you're new, take notes.
You're not so anxious that your own tone is leaking — your tone affects theirs (mirror neurons).

principles: Voss's three voices to DEPLOY: (1) Late-night FM DJ voice — slow, downward inflection, calm. Use to project authority and de-escalate. (2) Positive/playful — default 80% of the time. (3) Direct/assertive — rare, brief, for hard nos and hard limits only.
Pitch RISING at the end of a statement = uncertainty, even if the words are confident. The rep saying 'the price is two hundred?' (with rise) is asking your reaction.
Pitch FALLING firmly = conviction or finality. Falling-pitch 'no' is real; rising-pitch 'no' has room.
Pace ACCELERATING during a script = defensive recital. The rep wants to get through it and onto closure.
Pace SLOWING into a number = thinking, calculating, or fabricating. Slow + 'uhhh, let me see' before a number often means that number has 10-20% flex.
Throat-clearing, sigh, audible breath = state change. The rep just shifted internal position — could be giving in, could be hardening. The NEXT sentence reveals which.
Silence from the rep is data. 2-3 seconds = thinking; 5+ seconds = either consulting a system OR they want YOU to fill the silence. Don't.

phrases.match_their_dj_voice: (Lower your pitch, slow down to 60% pace, downward inflection.) 'I hear you. Let's see what we can do here.'
phrases.match_their_playful: (Slight upward energy, smile in your voice.) 'Ha, fair enough — let me think for a second.'
phrases.break_their_script: Quick question — off-script for a second — what would you do in my shoes?
phrases.address_hesitation: I can hear you weighing this — what would help you feel more certain?
phrases.address_finality: It sounds like that's a hard line for you. Can you help me understand where it comes from?
phrases.address_acceleration: We can slow down a bit — I want to make sure I'm tracking with you.

tactics.name: Default to the FM DJ voice in tense moments
tactics.when: Rep raises pitch, accelerates pace, or audibly stresses.
tactics.script: Drop YOUR pitch, slow YOUR pace. Within 30 seconds the rep's tone often syncs to yours (paralinguistic mirroring). Use `match_their_dj_voice`.

tactics.name: Mirror playful with playful
tactics.script: Match it — be warm and a little playful. People discount your asks by 10-20% when the call feels warm. Don't over-do it; you're not friends, but you're not adversaries either.

tactics.name: Use direct/assertive voice sparingly
tactics.when: You need to signal a true limit ('I cannot do month-to-month at that rate, full stop.').
tactics.script: Drop to lower pitch, sharper pace, no hedging language. Use ONCE per call max — overuse corrodes the FM DJ default.

tactics.name: Listen for the throat-clear before the number
tactics.when: Rep is about to deliver a price or term.
tactics.script: If you hear throat-clear, sigh, or 'uhhh' before the number — that number has flex. Mirror it back as a question (see `04-mirror-technique`) and wait.

tactics.name: Break the scripted cadence
tactics.when: Rep is delivering a long monotone block with no breaks.
tactics.script: At the next breath, use `break_their_script`. Interrupting the script gently breaks the rep out of compliance mode and into problem-solving mode.

decision_tree.description: Run this WHILE the rep is talking. Listen for paralinguistic patterns and route. The decision is made in the moment, not on review.

branches.if_you_hear: Rising pitch at the end of a 'no' or refusal.
branches.say: Calibrated question: 'What would need to change for that to work?'
branches.do_not: Do not accept the no as final. Pitch told you it wasn't.

branches.if_you_hear: Falling pitch at the end of a 'no'.
branches.say: Either pivot to a different dimension (see `08-multi-issue-tradeoffs`) or escalate authority.
branches.do_not: Do not press the same lever. The rep is firm on this specific ask.

branches.if_you_hear: Pace accelerating, especially during the rep's pricing recital.
branches.interpretation: Scripted, defensive. Trying to get through it.
branches.do_not: Do not let the script finish on its own terms. The script's design is to terminate negotiation.

branches.if_you_hear: Pace slowing, with 'uhhh' or 'let me see' before a number.
branches.interpretation: Either calculating or improvising. That number has flex.
branches.do_not: Do not assume the number is a system-derived hard limit. The hesitation tells you it isn't.

branches.interpretation: State change. Frustration, surrender, or recalibration.
branches.say: Label it: 'It sounds like that's a tough position to be in.' Then silence.
branches.do_not: Do not push immediately. The sigh is an opening — but only if you respect it.

branches.if_you_hear: Rep says 'honestly' / 'to be honest' / 'I'll level with you'.
branches.interpretation: Either real disclosure incoming OR a softening before a no. Listen carefully to the next sentence.
branches.say: Acknowledge briefly ('thanks for saying that') and listen.
branches.do_not: Do not interrupt. The next 10 seconds are high-information.

branches.if_you_hear: Rep's voice flattens — monotone, all energy gone.
branches.interpretation: Disengagement. Either tired, defeated, or has decided to stonewall.
branches.say: Inject warmth: 'I know I've been pushing — what would make this easier on your end?'
branches.do_not: Do not continue with the same tone. Mirror the flatness and the call dies.

branches.if_you_hear: Sudden warmth or playfulness from a previously stiff rep.
branches.interpretation: Genuine engagement. They've decided to help you.
branches.say: Match the warmth. Land your ask soon while the window is open.
branches.do_not: Do not stay stiff out of professional habit — meet them where they are.

branches.if_you_hear: Long silence (5+ seconds) after your ask, no typing/system sounds.
branches.interpretation: They are thinking, weighing, or hoping you'll fill the silence first.
branches.do_not: Do not soften your ask in the silence. Self-discounting is the most common amateur mistake.

branches.if_you_hear: Long silence with audible typing or clicking.
branches.interpretation: They are consulting a system. Genuine work. Good sign.
branches.say: Nothing. Wait. Maybe gentle 'take your time'.
branches.do_not: Do not interrupt the system look-up — they may be finding a discount code.

boundaries: Do NOT over-claim cue interpretation. Cues are probabilistic. Hold them lightly.
Do NOT mirror an angry tone — that escalates. Mirror calm, mirror warmth; do not mirror hostility.
Do NOT use the direct/assertive voice more than once per call.
Do NOT comment on the rep's tone aloud unless they bring it up first ('you sound stressed?' is patronizing).
success_signals: Rep's pitch drops toward your FM DJ baseline within 60 seconds of you using it.
Rep's pace slows to match yours.
Rep starts using disclosure markers ('honestly', 'between us').
Rep's voice warms after extended labeling — sincere engagement.
Rep audibly hesitates before a number you'd like to be flexible — and it is.
failure_signals: Rep's pitch keeps rising despite your calm tone — they may be in a real bind; back off.
Rep flattens to monotone and stays there — disengaged; consider escalation or callback.
You can't hear the cues — bad audio. Switch to a better line if possible.

exit_strategy.on_success: Once tone is synchronized and warm, land your ask. Tone-aligned asks succeed at much higher rates.
exit_strategy.on_failure: If you cannot establish tonal alignment in 5-10 minutes, the rep may have been instructed to maintain affect-neutral posture. Pivot to written follow-up where tone is less relevant.

required_user_info: User's natural speaking pace and pitch — for coaching them into the FM DJ voice if they are operating the call.
